title: Is it allowed to own a guard dog or service dog, and may one purchase a dog?
stanceSummary: Keeping dogs without need is strongly discouraged and affects rewards; dog saliva is najasah. The Qur'an allows hunting dogs; the Sunnah allows dogs for agriculture and shepherding. Many scholars extend this to any legitimate need—including guarding property or a medically needed assistance dog. Purchasing a dog: classical debate on the prohibition hadith; practical acquisition (adoption, nominal fee) is often discussed when purchase is problematic.
alternateQuestions: Is guard dog halal in islam?
Why can't Muslims have pet dogs?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eci9m7E6AFI) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eci9m7E6AFI&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi addresses this question.

- Pet dogs **without need** are **strongly discouraged**; **saliva** is **najis** for prayer purity.
- **Hunting, farming, herding** dogs are established; many scholars add **any legitimate need** (guard, disability).
- **Purchasing** dogs: classical tension with certain hadiths—scholars discuss **alternatives** to a direct sale.

---
